calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-13-2025 Set For Trial On Sep-28-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Aug-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SPEED's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS.

Plaintiff Speed's motion to quash subpoenas is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The subpoenas are enforceable as modified and limited by this order. (CCP 1987.1(a).)

(see word minutes for full details)

A proposed order is submitted prior to the hearing. Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Jun-02-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
LISA M. FLETCHER, ESQ. of BERG INJURY LAWYER Tel: (916) 641-5800 for palintiff.
IAN A. SCHAEFFER, ESQ. of MCNAMARA, AMBACHER,WHEELER, HIRSIG &GRAY LLP Tel: (707) 427-3998 for Defendant JESUS PANIAGUA.

Hearing is held.Defendant counsel contests the tentative ruling in its entirety as well as the sanctions awarded to plaintiff. The Matter argued. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to quash is granted.

Although personal injury plaintiffs implicitly waive their right to privacy, the waiver is not unlimited. (Davis v. Superior Ct. (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 1008, 1014 [holding that an implicit waiver of the right of privacy will encompass discovery "directly relevant" to the plaintiff's claims].) "[P]laintiffs . . . may not withhold information which relates to any physical or mental condition which they have put in issue by bringing th[e] lawsuit." (Britt v. Superior Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 864.)

Here, Defendant's subpoenas are clearly over broad as they seek "any and all" documents regarding Plaintiff's health information, billings, and employment with no time limitation. Defendant did not meet and confer in good faith. In a good faith effort to meet and confer with Defendant, Plaintiff sent e-mails on March 14 and 18 of 2025, to which Defendant failed to reply. (Fletcher Decl., par. 2.) Rather, after Plaintiff filed this motion on March 20, 2025, Defendant agreed to circumscribe its demands to relevant body parts and enter into a first look agreement. Defendant's efforts came too late. (CCP 1985.3(g); 1985.6(f)(4).)

The court does not find Defendant acted with substantial justification. Defendant to pay to Plaintiff $1,800 in sanctions within 10 days of service of notice of entry of order. (CCP 1987.2.)

If either party issues future subpoenas, the court suggests the parties actually meet and confer in good faith (including stipulating to a First Look Agreement) prior to coming to court.

Order is Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
